next_agent_suggestions: world-grid-author — can now write manifests per grid cell, following proof-of-life.manifest.json as template
location-builder — can adopt useScreenAssets() in scene components to load/unload per-location GLBs
asset-pipeline-3d (cycle 2) — wire in useKTX2 texture support; both useKTX2 and Ktx2 export cleanly from @react-three/drei v9 (verified this cycle)
notes:
Manifest schema: additionalProperties:true at the root and at every item level — downstream agents can add fields (e.g. lod_levels, collision_mesh, nav_mesh) without a schema-keeper failure. Screen identity is the only required field. All other arrays default to empty.

useScreenAssets design: preload() is called outside React render via a preloadedRef guard so it fires once per mount regardless of re-renders. The models map is built by calling useGLTF() per path inside the hook — callers must remount (key prop) if the manifest model-count changes between renders, which is the same constraint useGLTF carries itself. Returns { models: {id→gltf}, ready: boolean } where ready flips false if any result is missing.

Strict-mode double-mount: cleanup fires useGLTF.clear() after the first synthetic unmount, then the second mount calls preload()+useGLTF() again. drei/three caches by URL so the second resolve is near-instant from memory — no visible flicker. preloadedRef is reset in cleanup so the guard is correct across both mounts.

KTX2: useKTX2 exports cleanly from @react-three/drei v9.122 (confirmed grep on node_modules index). Skipped this cycle per spec — texture support is empty array reserved in the schema. No blocker.

Path layout: /game3d-assets/{models,textures,audio,manifests}/ — all absolute from public root so Vite serves correctly in dev and production. manifests/ directory already has a .gitkeep via the proof-of-life file itself.
